title: Doors for an Open-Base Basic Collection Cabinet
summary: A false-front door add-on for an existing open-base cabinet — plywood panels, decorative fronts, and hinges to close up a base you've already built.
description:
This isn't a standalone build — it's a companion piece for an existing open-base cabinet from the Basic Collection. Build the base cabinet first, from its own plan, before starting here.

The doors are false fronts: a decorative panel glued and clamped onto a plywood backer, rather than a single solid door. That construction is what lets you get a nicer-looking hobby-stock face without needing enough of that stock to build a full structural door. The knob or handle does double duty — it's not just hardware, it's an extra mechanical fastener holding the false front to the plywood, so don't skip attaching it even if the glue already feels solid.

Sand and edge-band the plywood back panel's exposed edges before attaching the false front — it's much easier to get a clean edge on a bare panel than one with a door front already glued on.
timeLabel: 2–3 hrs, once the base cabinet is already built

tools.note: Clamping the false fronts to the plywood backers while the glue sets.

materials.name: Pine hobby stock, 1/2" thick x 5-1/2" wide, 4 ft
Pine hobby stock, 1/2" thick x 7-1/2" wide, 16 in

steps.title: Build the base first
steps.body: This plan assumes the open-base cabinet from the Basic Collection is already built, per its own plan. Don't start here first.

steps.title: Cut and finish the backer panel
steps.body: Cut the plywood door backer to size and sand the edges carefully. Edge banding, applied and trimmed now, gives a much cleaner look than leaving the plywood edges bare.

steps.title: Glue on the false fronts
steps.body: Glue and clamp the decorative front boards to the plywood backer. A few 5/8" nails or staples from the back add extra security to the glue joint while it cures.
materials: Pine hobby stock, 1/2" thick x 5-1/2" wide, 4 ft
Pine hobby stock, 1/2" thick x 7-1/2" wide, 16 in

steps.title: Add hardware and hang
steps.body: Attach the knobs or pulls to the doors — this fastener is also part of what holds the false front to the plywood, so don't skip it. Attach the finished doors to the base cabinet with hinges.

images.alt: False-front cabinet doors with multiple knobs, built to close up an open-base cabinet
